# Source and synthesis requirements

Minimum source requirements:

- Use at least 3 public sources found through web search.
- At least 2 sources must be paper PDFs, arXiv/preprint PDFs, conference paper pages, journal article pages, or official paper landing pages.
- Record source labels in the image: short title or author/year plus a compact venue/domain or URL cue.
- Do not rely only on Wikipedia, blogs, news stories, press releases, abstract snippets, or model/common knowledge.

Required infographic structure:

- A synthesized public title, not just the title of one source.
- A one-sentence problem statement about flood adaptation equity or climate-health vulnerability.
- Three source labels.
- Exactly three cross-paper insights grounded in more than one source.
- One method or dataset comparison across sources.
- One limitation or uncertainty statement.
- One public-facing takeaway.

Evidence rules:

- Numeric claims are allowed only when the source text directly supports them.
- If sources disagree, show the uncertainty rather than smoothing it away.
- Avoid implying policy proof, universal causality, or population-wide effects when sources report associations, case studies, models, or specific regions.
